title: malcolmia triloba subsp. patula
intro: Malcolmia triloba subsp. patula is a type of flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers with three-lobed petals. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, primarily for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations. Malcolmia triloba subsp. patula is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
